matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules also receive the actual sample REF -> ALT change; curated allele-change rules take precedence when the observed ALT allele or exact change has a known direction.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: rs2736100 (intronic TERT cancer- and telomere-associated SNP)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a telomere-maintenance thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of telomere length, replicative lifespan, stem-cell maintenance, promoter activity, or cancer susceptibility because TERT encodes the catalytic telomerase subunit. This predicts telomere-biology context, not a direct cancer diagnosis.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: intronic TERT cancer- and telomere-associated SNP
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs2736100 suggests this sample carries the TERT prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a telomere-maintenance thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of telomere length, replicative lifespan, stem-cell maintenance, promoter activity, or cancer susceptibility because TERT encodes the catalytic telomerase subunit. This predicts telomere-biology context, not a direct cancer diagnosis. This specific marker is curated as intronic TERT cancer- and telomere-associated SNP. rs2736100 is one of the best-known common TERT SNPs and is curated here as a telomere-biology and cancer-susceptibility marker. It is not treated as a pathogenic diagnostic allele in the local database.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs2736100 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the TERT prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Commonly interpreted through telomere-maintenance and cancer-risk frameworks.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: Observed rs2853669 suggests this sample carries the TERT prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a telomere-maintenance thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of telomere length, replicative lifespan, stem-cell maintenance, promoter activity, or cancer susceptibility because TERT encodes the catalytic telomerase subunit. This predicts telomere-biology context, not a direct cancer diagnosis. This specific marker is curated as promoter regulatory TERT SNP. rs2853669 is a promoter-region TERT polymorphism frequently discussed as a modifier of promoter activity and cancer outcomes. The local database treats it as a research-level promoter modifier rather than as a pathogenic allele.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs2853669 with the actual DNA change {change}. Anchor the TERT prediction to that observed REF -> ALT result instead of treating the variant as only a theoretical marker. The current preview does not infer zygosity or allele dose, so dosage-specific conclusions require genotype fields.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Promoter-facing regulatory variant often modeled as a transcription-factor binding modifier.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a telomere-maintenance thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of telomere length, replicative lifespan, stem-cell maintenance, promoter activity, or cancer susceptibility because TERT encodes the catalytic telomerase subunit. This predicts telomere-biology context, not a direct cancer diagnosis. An observed TERT variant places this sample in the curated TERT research context. The local TERT knowledge base spans telomere biology, cancer susceptibility, and promoter regulation. Common TERT variants are interpreted as research and risk-modifier signals, while the strongest clinical events often involve promoter mutations or rare high-impact pathogenic alleles. Common TERT polymorphisms are often interpreted through telomere-length, cancer-risk, or promoter-activity frameworks rather than as stand-alone diagnostic variants.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: TERT is central to telomere biology, cellular senescence, stem-cell maintenance, and oncogenesis.
Common germline SNPs and somatic promoter mutations are both heavily studied in cancer biology, but they represent different interpretive layers.
Cancer and telomerase-reactivation studies.

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a TERT variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a telomere-maintenance thesis: the individual may carry a modifier of telomere length, replicative lifespan, stem-cell maintenance, promoter activity, or cancer susceptibility because TERT encodes the catalytic telomerase subunit. This predicts telomere-biology context, not a direct cancer diagnosis. TERT methylation is best interpreted as promoter and chromatin context around telomerase regulation. It can inform whether the locus appears permissive or repressed, but it is not a direct substitute for telomere-length or promoter-mutation testing. The local TERT knowledge base spans telomere biology, cancer susceptibility, and promoter regulation. Common TERT variants are interpreted as research and risk-modifier signals, while the strongest clinical events often involve promoter mutations or rare high-impact pathogenic alleles. Common TERT polymorphisms are often interpreted through telomere-length, cancer-risk, or promoter-activity frameworks rather than as stand-alone diagnostic variants.
